subject: First Aid & Safety
title: Heatstroke: The Emergency
description: A quick win: understand Heatstroke: The Emergency.

hook.text: Heatstroke kills the brain at 106°F. It is the deadliest heat emergency with a 10-50% fatality rate.

buildup.text: Core temperature exceeds 104°F and the body's cooling system has completely failed.

discovery.text: The skin is hot, red, and DRY—sweating has stopped. Confusion, seizures, and unconsciousness follow.

twist.text: Organ damage begins within minutes. Every minute without cooling increases mortality.

climax.text: Aggressive cooling—cold water immersion—is the gold standard treatment before EMS arrives.

punchline.text: Heatstroke is a medical emergency. Cool first, call 911 second. Minutes matter.

quiz.question: What is a critical sign that heat exhaustion has progressed to heatstroke?
options: The person stops sweating and skin becomes hot and dry
